MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Micheal Fitzergerald and Eric Francisconi for and with Debra Duncan and for Nicole Biddinger (Petitioners/Objectors).
Christopher Land for Catherine Lewallen (Respondent).
John Boessenecker for Ramon Apodaca (Respondent).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. Upon conclusion of argument, the Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied in part and granted in part. The tentative ruling as to the first and second causes of action are confirmed for the reasons stated in the tentative ruling: The Motion for Summary Adjudication filed by Respondent Catherine Lewallen and Respondent Ramon Apodaca III (collectively, "Respondents") is denied in part. With respect to the first two causes of action addressed, that is, Petitioners Nicole Biddinger and Debra Duncan's ("Petitioners") claim under Probate Code 850 and related claim for declaratory relief, Respondents have failed to make a prima facie showing that there are no triable issues of material fact as to the 850 claim and thus have failed to shift the burden of production onto Petitioners. Excluding direct evidence does not exclude the possibility that Petitioners could provide circumstantial evidence of the property belonging in trust. Even if Respondents had shifted the burden of production, there are triable issues of fact regarding Decedent's knowledge and intent with respect to the property, namely whether he intended to return title to the trust in light of the facts that he, e.g., deposited the funds obtained through the reverse mortgage process into the trust account and continued to pay expenses related to the property from that fund until his death. See, e.g. Petitioners' Additional Undisputed Material Facts. Thus, Respondents' request for summary adjudication of the first cause of action is denied. Because the second cause of action for declaratory relief simply seeks a declaration from the Court that presumes success on the 850 claim, summary adjudication of that claim is also denied, for the reasons stated above. As to the third cause of action for Quiet Title, the Court granted the third cause of action for summary adjudication finding that the quiet title action has not been adequately plead and it would be futile to allow an opportunity to amend for reasons discussed. Mr. Fitzgerald and Mr. Francisconi are to prepare a form of order and provide it to opposing Counsel for approval as to form.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Reporter: Mary J. Visciglio, CSR #10391, 415-591-3333.

Off Calendar Oct-17-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-299977 The D J H Trust and PES-17-300524 Estate of James T. Pongracz.

Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for Alyson Tufts.
Randall Boessenecker for Raymond Apodaca.
Christopher Land for Catherine Lewellan.
Michael Fitzgerald appeared by telephone for Debra Duncan.

Ms. Lofdahl stated that they have done two mediations and that neither was fruitful. Mr. Fitzgerald asked to have until November 3rd to file the amended 850 Petition, stated that they have a critical deposition of Ms. Lewellan set, that another mediation session may be helpful, that he has completed more than 50% of his discovery and that they still have an issue in terms of what is properly before the Court. Mr. Boessenecker stated that they will be demurring to any amended petition. Mr. Land and Mr. Boessenecker stated that they have no objection to the appointment of Ms. Lofdahl's Client as Successor Trustee. Mr. Fitzgerald stated that he continues to lodge conditional objections, that he does not want to bring new parties in, and noted that funds are now in a blocked account. The Court granted Ms. Lofdahl's Petition for Appointment of Successor Trustee with bond (subject to receipt and review of the order). Ms. Lofdahl is to determine the appropriate of amount of bond or ask the parties if they are willing to waive bond. Status on filing of bond is set for October 31, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Fitzgerald's petition for appointment of Successor Trustee is denied. Both petitions for declaratory relief, etc. are continued to December 12, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the expectation that the parties have moved things along, and have met and conferred.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Demurrer To First Amended Probate Code Sec. 850
Off Calendar Oct-04-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The matter was called with case no. PES-17-300524, The Estate of James T. Pongracz.

Appearances: George Vausher and Michael Fitzgerald for Debra Duncan and Nicole Biddinger (Petitioners). Christopher Land for Catherine Lewallen (Respondent/Beneficiary). John Boessnecker for Ramon Apodaca III (Respondent).

Matters called. Mr. Fitzgerald stated that he would present argument regarding the Demurrer in the Trust matter, that he would politely disagree with the Court in the Estate matter, that he would "table" the tort claims, and that he would be limiting his remarks/argument to the Trust case. Argument held. Mr. Land stated that that the tentative ruling was clear. At the conclusion of argument, the Court stated that it would adopt the tentative ruling - that the demurrer would be sustained with leave to amend. The Court then adopted the tentative ruling as modified: Overruled as to the property claim causes of action 1-3 (850, quiet title, and declaratory relief) and cause of action 12 (for an accounting). Sustained with 30 days leave to amend as to all other causes of action for lack of standing since Petitioners have no interest in Pongracz' property except through the terms of the trust. If the Court determines the property that is the subject of the 850 claim belongs in the trust, they will receive their full share of it. If the Court determines it does not belong to the trust, the Court will necessarily have determined that no compensable injury was done to them by its removal from the trust. They are not otherwise succesors in interest or interested persons under the statutes to which they refer, and they have suggested no basis on which they could amend their petition to change that result. Mr. Boessnecker is to prepare a form of order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Alesia L. Collins-Hudson, CSR #7751, of Esquire Deposition Solutions, Tel. 415.591.3333.
